questions.question: According to RFC 5881 §5, if BFD is configured with a transmit interval of 50 ms and a detection multiplier of 3, what is the worst-case failure detection time?
options: 50 ms
100 ms
150 ms
300 ms
questions.explanation: Detection time = DetectMult × max(DesiredMinTxInterval, RequiredMinRxInterval). With 50 ms and multiplier 3, detection time = 3 × 50 ms = 150 ms.

questions.question: What is the key advantage of TI-LFA (RFC 9355) over classic LFA (RFC 5286) on a ring topology?
options: TI-LFA uses shorter BFD intervals
TI-LFA achieves 100% backup coverage using segment-routing paths, while LFA covers only ~50%
TI-LFA eliminates the need for BFD entirely
TI-LFA requires no pre-computation of backup paths
questions.explanation: Classic LFA can only protect a link if a loop-free alternate neighbor exists; on a ring this is roughly 50% of links. TI-LFA uses a pre-computed SR segment list following the post-convergence topology, achieving 100% coverage on any topology.

questions.question: In the discrete-event simulator, what sequence of events models a single FRR-protected link failure?
options: LINK_DOWN → IGP_SPF → FRR_SWITCH → TRIAL_END
LINK_DOWN → BFD_TIMEOUT → FRR_SWITCH → TRIAL_END
BFD_TIMEOUT → LINK_DOWN → FRR_SWITCH → TRIAL_END
LINK_DOWN → FRR_SWITCH → BFD_TIMEOUT → TRIAL_END
questions.explanation: The timing chain is: physical failure (LINK_DOWN at t=0), BFD detects the failure after interval × multiplier (BFD_TIMEOUT), the line card reprograms the backup next-hop (FRR_SWITCH), then TRIAL_END records total convergence time.

questions.question: When a link has no FRR backup (strategy='none' or an uncovered LFA link), what determines the recovery time in the simulator?
options: BFD detection time alone (150 ms)
A sampled IGP convergence time modelled as Gaussian with mean 800 ms
The FRR_SWITCH activation constant of 10 ms
The BFD multiplier times the interval plus a fixed 50 ms penalty
questions.explanation: Without a pre-programmed backup, the router must fall back to IGP re-convergence (IS-IS SPF delay, RFC 7665). The simulator models this as a Gaussian distribution with mean 800 ms and standard deviation 120 ms, lower-bounded at 200 ms.

questions.question: Why does reducing BFD intervals from 50 ms × 3 to 10 ms × 3 carry operational risk in production networks?
options: It makes BFD incompatible with IS-IS and OSPF
It increases the chance of false positives during congestion; Miercom 2024 found 4 of 7 platforms miss 10 ms × 3 under 80% line-rate load
It causes TI-LFA to lose 100% coverage
It conflicts with RFC 9355 which mandates 50 ms minimum
questions.explanation: Shorter BFD intervals mean more CPU overhead and sensitivity to transient packet loss under load. The Miercom 2024 BFD test report documented that the majority of tested platforms miss sub-10 ms intervals under heavy traffic, causing spurious link-down events.

questions.question: In the ring30 topology, what characteristic gives rLFA (~85% coverage) an advantage over classic LFA (~50% coverage)?
options: rLFA uses a shorter BFD multiplier
rLFA tunnels traffic to a remote PQ node that can reach the destination without using the failed link, bypassing the topological constraint that limits local LFA
rLFA pre-installs SR labels instead of IP next-hops
rLFA relies on ECMP to redistribute flows automatically
questions.explanation: Remote LFA (RFC 7490) extends coverage by computing a PQ node — a node reachable from both the PLR and the destination without traversing the failed link — and tunneling traffic there. This resolves cases where no local neighbor satisfies the LFA inequality.
